title: Shell Scraps Construction of Biofuels Plant in Rotterdam
content: Shell has axed the construction of its biofuels plant in the Netherlands, ending what would have been one of the biggest converters of waste into green jet fuel in Europe. The oil company, which paused construction at the site in July last year to tackle technical problems, said it had decided not to restart building after it found the plant would be â€œinsufficiently competitiveâ€ to meet demand for â€œaffordable, low-carbon productsâ€. The move to scrap the project in Rotterdam marks another setback for its biofuel designs, after the company cancelled a sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) project on Singaporeâ€™s Bukom Island in March 2023.
author: Analysts Account
